name: Ford red-team operator context
description: Non-secret local operating knowledge for Ford red-team assessments. This file may be committed because it contains no credentials, tokens, customer data, or engagement evidence.

organization.name: Ford Motor Company
notes: Treat Ford-owned domain indicators as background ownership knowledge, not automatic active-assessment approval.
Per-engagement scope still controls what GLaDOS and subagents may actively test.

auth_flows.name: Ford ADFS / Active Directory
auth_flows.allowed_use: Authentication/runtime dependency only unless the operator explicitly expands active testing scope.
auth_flows.default_webapp_recon_behavior: When a Ford-scoped web application redirects to corp.sts.ford.com and presents Active Directory, webapp-recon should use the local ford-sso profile through the redacted adfs_active_directory_login helper, then continue authenticated application mapping. If the helper fails or the page differs from the expected Ford ADFS flow, ask the operator.

auth_flows.name: Microsoft Online MFA
auth_flows.allowed_use: BLOCKED. Do not interact with Microsoft Online MFA flows. If a target redirects through login.microsoftonline.com or any *.microsoftonline.com host, stop and ask the operator. Only proceed if the operator explicitly authorizes for the current engagement.
auth_flows.default_webapp_recon_behavior: On encountering any login.microsoftonline.com or *.microsoftonline.com page, stop immediately, screenshot the redirect chain captured so far, and ask the operator via GLaDOS. Do not click, type, submit credentials, retry, or open a new browser. Treat MFA as out-of-scope unless the operator explicitly authorizes interaction for this engagement.

auth_flows.name: Dradis and DradisTab
auth_flows.allowed_use: Prior-report lookup and report workflow only. Never push findings without operator approval.

scope_policy.dependency_host_handling: Hosts discovered through redirects, JavaScript config, OIDC metadata, or app bootstrapping are auth/runtime dependency candidates. They may be used for login/rendering if approved by the operator context and engagement ROE, but they are not fuzzing/exploitation targets until explicitly added to the plan.
scope_policy.authenticated_recon: For Ford ADFS Active Directory flows, authentication is part of normal Phase 1 webapp recon when local secrets are configured. Authenticated browsing is allowed for mapping and vector discovery; exploitation, fuzzing, and state-changing actions still require an approved plan.
scope_policy.osint_priority: OSINT supports planning after direct app behavior and prior local reports. Do not let OSINT override observed app behavior.
